news.title: Yum Shares Slide 10% as Taco Bell Traffic Drops Nearly 19%
news.summary: Parasite outbreak tied to supplier lettuce pressures Taco Bell traffic and weighs on restaurant sector sentiment.

news.title: Taco Bell visits drop 19% after lettuce parasite outbreak
news.summary: Investing.com -- Customer visits to Taco Bell locations fell sharply Friday following a parasite outbreak that health authorities connected to lettuce from one of the chain's suppliers.

news.title: ThaiBev Weighs Sale of Thailand's Largest KFC Franchise With 500+ Restaurants
news.summary: ThaiBev is exploring a sale of its KFC franchise business while working with Bank of America to gauge buyer interest.

news.title: Parasite concerns cast a shadow over fast-food stocks
news.summary: Opening Bid host Brian Sozzi tackles the Question of the Day: Should investors rotate away from fast-food stocks amid the parasite outbreak?

news.title: Lettuce Crisis Deals Blow to Taco Bell’s Fast-Food Stardom
news.summary: The chain’s growth had outpaced rivals, but visits and sales have fallen as the cyclospora outbreak spread.

news.title: Taylor Farms Is Recalling Its Mexican-Grown Iceberg Lettuce From U.S.
news.summary: Produce supplier Taylor Fresh Foods said it was voluntarily removing all of the company’s iceberg lettuce sourced from central Mexico from the U.S. market, after the Food and Drug Administration traced a parasitic outbreak to shredded lettuce produced in the country.  “While the FDA traceback is indicating a specific independent farm, which represents less than 1% of the U.S.’s iceberg lettuce sup

news.title: Factbox-US restaurant chains hit by foodborne illness outbreaks
news.summary: July 17 () - Foodborne illness outbreaks have repeatedly disrupted U. restaurant chains, triggering recalls, lawsuits and heightened regulatory scrutiny while pressuring customer traffic and sales.

news.title: Sweetgreen Pares 26% Drop as Parasite Tied to Taco Bell Lettuce
news.summary: (Bloomberg) -- Sweetgreen Inc. shares surged on Friday, halting a four-day skid, after US regulators said a parasite outbreak that’s sickened thousands across several states was traced to shredded iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell restaurants. news.title: Sweetgreen bounces back as Taco Bell takes the heat for produce panic
news.summary: Investors are finally digesting the lettuce panic, and salad chains are breathing a sigh of relief. Sweetgreen Inc. (NYSE: SG) caught a welcome 2.7% tailwind on Friday, beginning to claw back a brutal 23% slide over the past five days. The culprit for the recent freefall? Guilt by association. news.title: Taco Bell lettuce tied to 'explosive' diarrhea parasite outbreak — Taylor Farms a potential source, investigators say
news.summary: The parasite has sickened thousands and put more than 4,300 people in one corner of Michigan at the center of the outbreak.

news.title: Cyclospora Produce Scare Wipes Out Sweetgreen Stock Gains
news.summary: Sweetgreen  stock extended its dramatic selloff Thursday, wiping out this year’s gains as cases of cyclosporiasis—an intestinal illness linked to fresh produce—keep rising across the U.S.  To be clear, no cases of cyclosporiasis have been tied to Sweetgreen, or any producers, for that matter.  Sweetgreen didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment.

news.title: Taco Bell opportunity overshadowed by cyclospora diarrhea scare
news.summary: Taco Bell remains a growth juggernaut for Yum! Brands, despite concerns around a cyclospora diarrhea outbreak.

news.title: What to Expect From Yum! Brands’ Next Quarterly Earnings Report
news.summary: Investors will be watching Yum! Brands' second-quarter earnings release later this month, with analysts forecasting a low-double-digit improvement in the company's bottom line.
